patch.value: Least-privilege capability reconciliation: pm package doctor flags declared-but-unused extension capabilities + SDK assertExtensionCapabilityUsage

Add the inverse of the existing missing-capability enforcement (pm-mwwp): detect capabilities a package manifest DECLARES but never EXERCISES at activation, so authors can trim manifests to least privilege. Surfaced as advisory extension_capability_unused warnings in pm package/extension doctor, plus a batteries-included SDK test helper assertExtensionCapabilityUsage so authors catch over-declaration at npm test time. Closes the least-privilege loop opened by pm-bav0 (scaffold least-privilege metadata) and pm-mwwp (enforce missing capabilities).

author: claude-code-agent
message: Link related capability/least-privilege items for graph richness

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
Implemented least-privilege capability reconciliation across core + doctor + SDK:

CORE (src/core/extensions/capability-usage.ts, new):
- EXTENSION_CAPABILITY_REGISTRATION_SURFACES: canonical capability->surface map, `satisfies Record<ExtensionCapability,...>` makes it exhaustive at COMPILE TIME (adding a capability without a surface fails the build, preventing a perpetually-"unused" false positive).
- collectUsedExtensionCapabilities(activation, {extensionName?}): unions exercised capabilities, optional case-insensitive name filter.
- reconcileExtensionCapabilityUsage(loaded, activation): per-extension {declared, used, unused}; filters declared to KNOWN capabilities (unknowns are the separate unknown-capability diagnostic); skips extensions with no reconcilable declared caps. Keyed by JSON [layer,name] tuple (no literal NUL in source).

DOCTOR (extension/doctor.ts + extension.ts):
- classifyUnusedCapabilityWarnings(loadResult, activationResult) emits advisory `extension_capability_unused:<layer>:<name>:<capability>`; wired into the doctor warning pipeline (NOT install-probe).
- buildExtensionTriageSummary adds a least-privilege remediation branch.
- ADVISORY only: doctor `ok` stays true (status:"warn"). Deliberately NOT wired into `pm health` (isAdvisoryHealthWarning only whitelists telemetry_*/history_* -> any extension warning would block the health gate + daily release). Verified pm health unaffected.

SDK (sdk/testing.ts + sdk/index.ts + core/extensions/index.ts):
- assertExtensionCapabilityUsage(activation, {declared, extensionName?, allowUnused?}) — package-test counterpart; throws on over-declaration (singular/plural phrasing), rejects unknown declared caps. Barrel-re-exported + barrel-equality locked.
- Exported collectUsedExtensionCapabilities / reconcileExtensionCapabilityUsage / EXTENSION_CAPABILITY_REGISTRATION_SURFACES from the SDK.

VERIFICATION: 4807 tests pass, 100/100/100/100 coverage; typecheck + quality:static (docstrings) + docs-skills all green. Repo's own 10 first-party packages + builtins are already least-privilege (0 warnings). Sandbox dogfood: over-declaring ["commands","schema","search"] with only a command -> flagged schema+search; trim to ["commands"] -> clean.

patch.value: Shipped least-privilege capability reconciliation: advisory extension_capability_unused doctor warnings + assertExtensionCapabilityUsage SDK helper + collectUsedExtensionCapabilities/reconcileExtensionCapabilityUsage core exports. 100% coverage, all gates green.
